summary: A layout prose region declared as an import resolves to its target file's contents — fingerprinted in the lock, a declared graph edge, refusing loudly when dangling

new.description: An import-declared prose region resolves to its target file's contents; the dependency is fingerprinted in the lock; a dangling target refuses before a byte is written (pipeline.md, Emit: "Refusing"); the edge appears in the same resolved enumeration explain/impact narrate.

edit.description: Thread the import region's resolution through emit and the gate: refusal when dangling, the resolved edge into the one enumeration the gate and read verbs share (contract.md, edge).

The import target's fingerprint reaches the lock — a dependency the lock fingerprints, so a moved target is drift in the one disk-versus-lock vocabulary. `derive_layout_rows` (the LAYOUT-READER pass reading a layout document, drift.rs:553) is where the layout's regions already surface engine-side.
The import edge joins the resolved edge set the gate, explain, and impact already range over — path-resolved against raw disk, never the ignore-filtered discovery view (contract.md, edge).

tests.asserts: an import region resolves and is fingerprinted; a dangling import refuses before any write; the edge is narrated by the read verbs

acceptance: A layout prose region declared as an import resolves, is fingerprinted in the committed lock, and appears as a declared edge to the gate and read verbs; a dangling import refuses loudly before any byte is written; all gates green.
notes: Scoped at a2279cf (0019); unblocked at e3982b5 — LAYOUT-READER shipped (kind.rs Layout::read; its commit names imports out of scope). Kin to the composed-prose include (pipeline.md, The SDK): if one resolution surface honestly serves both, unifying them is the sanctioned consolidation (engineering.md), named in the commit body — never creep. SDK shape landed in KIND-CONTENT-FACT (LayoutRegionRow import ref); this entry is engine-side resolve + fingerprint + edge.

summary: Consolidate the layout reader's duplicated test suites — the four Layout::read behaviors and their intent fixtures assert once, in tests/layout_kind.rs

edit.description: Retire the #[cfg(test)] duplicates of the reader suite — `read_fills_field_slots_verbatim_and_collects_members_by_slugged_heading` (kind.rs:1520), `an_explicit_key_survives_a_heading_retitle` (1547), `a_missing_declared_section_refuses_loud_naming_file_and_heading` (1576), and the duplicated `intent_layout()` (1491) / `INTENT_DOC` (1506) fixtures. `an_unadmitted_top_level_heading_refuses_loud` (1590) moves to the integration suite rather than being dropped — it is the one reader behavior asserted nowhere else. The rest of the test module (overlay/lift tests) stays.

The one home for the reader's behavior tests: absorb the unadmitted-top-level-heading refusal beside the existing reader tests (`the_reader_fills_slots_collects_members_and_lands_prose_verbatim`, `an_explicit_key_survives_a_heading_retitle`, `a_document_missing_a_declared_section_fails_loud_naming_file_and_heading`); the emit-face tests stay as-is.

tests.asserts: all four reader behaviors — slot fill + member collection + verbatim prose, explicit-key retitle survival, missing-section refusal, unadmitted-heading refusal — in the single integration suite

acceptance: Layout::read's behavior is asserted in exactly one suite (tests/layout_kind.rs), the unadmitted-heading refusal included; src/kind.rs's test module carries no duplicate reader tests or intent fixtures; all gates green.
notes: Scoped at 1284cdf (residue sweep of e3982b5): the reader landed with near-verbatim twin suites — same fixture, same public Layout::read — in src/kind.rs unit tests and tests/layout_kind.rs. Keep the integration home (it already holds the emit face); extract.rs's unit tests stay (they pin pub(crate) internals unreachable from the integration crate). Disjoint from LAYOUT-PROSE-IMPORT — no shared file; resolution is engine-side per kind.rs's Prose-arm comment.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, still at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Re-verified at 5819ac5 (ship audit): still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
